topic.what: Web tracking practices across ten countries and impact of privacy laws on tracker connections
topic.how: Automated crawling of 9,975 sites (Common Top 525 and Country-specific Top 525) from VM servers in each country, measuring tracker entries via Privacy Pioneer extension
topic.why_matters: Geographic location significantly impacts user tracker exposure; privacy law enforcement varies widely and affects real-world privacy outcomes

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of HTTP tracking entries per site across advertising, analytics, and social categories
characteristics.context: Common Top 525 sites crawled from each country; US and Australia are opt-out jurisdictions; Germany and Spain are opt-in with high enforcement

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage reduction in tracker connections when accessing Common Top 525 sites from EU vs non-EU countries
characteristics.context: GDPR and ePrivacy Directive effect measured across Germany and Spain vs non-EU countries

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction in tracker connections when not interacting with cookie banners vs consenting
characteristics.context: Sample of 36 Common Top 525 sites; advertising trackers reduced most in Germany (51.7% advertising, 34.5% analytics, 58.1% social)

characteristics.what_itasures: Percentage of sites successfully crawled across all countries
characteristics.context: 9 Country-specific Top 525 lists plus 1 Common Top 525 list; 5250 unique sites total

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of Common Top 525 sites showing cookie banners across all ten countries
characteristics.vs_baseline: Not showing banners in any country: 23% (97/420)
characteristics.context: Indicates moderate Brussels effect; 68/72 two-country sites deploy banners only in Germany and Spain

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of Country-specific Top 525 sites making zero tracker connections
characteristics.context: Significant variation across countries; Germany highest protection; Australia/Brazil/US lowest

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total GDPR fines issued in EU for insufficient legal basis for data processing
characteristics.context: As of March 2026; Germany and Spain are high enforcement jurisdictions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ratio of advertising tracker connections to analytics tracker connections
characteristics.context: Advertising trackers need more frequent connections for user profiling; analytics only need periodic connections

good_for: comparing privacy law effectiveness across jurisdictions
requires: Privacy Pioneer or similar tracker detection

novelty: first multi-country measurement (10 countries, all continents) comparing Common Top 525 and Country-specific Top 525 site tracking
quantified EU law effect as Brussels shield vs Brussels effect
measured country-specific enforcement impact on real-world tracking
compared opt-in vs opt-out jurisdiction outcomes with matched site sets
open_problems: longitudinal tracking of privacy law enforcement evolution
integration of Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals
web standards for native privacy enforcement
closing compliance gaps in Brazil, South Korea, India despite strong privacy laws
